title: Jayson Tatum, frustrated by shooting slump, is showing both rust and flashes
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum struggles with shooting after returning from a serious injury, shooting 38.8% from the field.
Despite Tatum's struggles, the Celtics have won six of their first eight games with him back in the lineup.
Tatum is focusing on mental aspects to regain form as Celtics nearing playoffs, with 11 regular season games remaining.

title: Bones Hyland, short-handed Timberwolves beat ice-cold Celtics: 6 takeaways
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Timberwolves, led by Bones Hyland's 23 points, defeated Celtics 102-92, despite missing Anthony Edwards.
Celtics had a scoring drought in the fourth quarter, allowing Timberwolves a decisive 16-point run.
Jayson Tatum struggled, shooting 6-for-16, while Jaylen Brown's 29 points couldn't overcome late game misses.

title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump considers deploying ICE agents to Boston's Logan Airport amid ongoing government shutdown.
Over 400 TSA workers quit after working without pay, prompting consideration of ICE deployment at airports.
Logan Airport has not experienced the delays seen at other hubs and Massport reports no impacts to checkpoints.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman delivered a standout performance, stopping 42 of 44 shots, leading Boston to a critical 4-2 victory over Detroit.
Despite Boston's defense ranking last in expected goals against per 60 minutes, Swayman boasts strong goaltending numbers this season.
Swayman is second in the NHL in goals saved above expected at 27.1, bolstering his candidacy for the Vezina Trophy.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as a legitimate NBA MVP candidate for the current basketball season.
Jaylen Brown is averaging career-highs with 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game this season.
Boston Celtics are in second place in the Eastern Conference, largely due to Brown's exceptional performance.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave due to a criminal investigation into her off-duty conduct.
Massachusetts State Police launched the investigation, and Plymouth police confirmed it is ongoing without providing further details.
Pelrine joined the Plymouth police in April 2022; the department commits to accountability amid its internal investigation.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: MLB writer Eno Sarris predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP in 2026, highlighting his improved swing path.
Anthony impressed in 2025 with the Red Sox, recording a .292 average, eight home runs, and an .859 OPS over 71 games.
The 21-year-old elevated his stock at the 2026 World Baseball Classic, batting .280 with two homers and seven RBI for Team USA.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run on Friday morning near Southampton and Bradston streets in Roxbury.
The victim was taken to a nearby hospital where they were pronounced dead, according to Boston police.
The driver fled the scene; the vehicle was found later, but no arrests have been made as investigations continue.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about a wintry mix through Monday night
author: Jack Pickell and Heather Alterisio
bullets: Meteorologists forecast rain transitioning to snow across the region, with potential travel impacts mainly north of I-90.
Scattered snow showers and squalls may create slippery spots tonight as temperatures drop into the 20s.
Minimal snow accumulation is expected, with up to 1 inch possible on non-paved surfaces in higher elevations.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood police officer Shawn Willman arrested Thursday for operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol.
Officer Willman was off duty during the arrest and has been placed on administrative leave.
Norwood Police Department conducting internal affairs investigation; Willman's employment depends on criminal case outcome.
